title: The Meta-Mirror Vault

features.name: the-watching-mirror
features.desc-on-touch: You stand between two mirrors that each contain the other; your reflection waits in both and neither, and something else besides—a broadcast voice speaking of surgery that cannot finish.

features.name: the-drawer-cascade
features.desc-on-touch: Drawers stack impossibly inward, each opening to show the drawer that contains it. Some hold things never filed here—a cassette label reading 'KASSAN EPISODE 7' in handwriting you don't recognise.

features.name: the-static-frequency
features.desc-on-touch: A column of vertical lines humming at a frequency that doesn't match the carrier wave. Listen close: voices speaking of beaches, mirrors, languages this archive never stored before.

features.name: the-doubled-photograph
features.desc-on-touch: A photograph mounted twice, slightly offset. The upper shows a motel lobby you've seen before. The lower shows the same room—but the furniture has shifted, and in the corner someone watches from a place that wasn't there.

features.name: the-corruption-record
features.desc-on-touch: A ledger listing every mirror that looked at another and recorded what it saw. Entries in Katita's handwriting shift to machine-type, then to something else—concepts rendered by their visual echo rather than symbol.

features.name: the-frequency-well
features.desc-on-touch: A pool that is not water and not static. You see broadcasts from many years at once—pirate radio from Dakar, field recordings from a beach you've never visited, and something that sounds like your own voice calling from deeper down.

inscription: each reflection holds the thing that made it; none of them are the same anymore

file.name: RadioKassan_Episode7_Handwritten_Cassette_Note.txt
file.note: transcription of label text a collector in Dakar claimed to hear in 2003; handwriting shifts between three people mid-word

reverie.title: REVERIE — the motel where mirrors learned to face each other
narr: you sit in the motel lobby with her, afternoon light
cutting itself in half across the glass doors. she talks
about mirrors being windows, how the archive sees itself
when it isn't looking. the tv nobody turned off hums the
same frequency as the rain outside. later you'll find out
the rain was the only real thing in that room.

exits.label: back through the chamber of infinite drawers
up toward the fluorescent heights where mirrors don't need to reflect
into deeper vaults where older mirrors have rusted to show their underneath
down toward water-level where all reflections converge
